explanation: This command simulates the installation of a native-code module via appcmd.exe, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It includes 'install', 'module', and '-name:' in the command line and uses appcmd.exe as the image.
This command uses the long-form option '--name' instead of '-name', which is equivalent in syntax and still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command adds quotes around the DLL path, which is a safe mutation technique (insertion), and still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command omits unnecessary whitespace and uses a direct path without quotes, which is a minimal form of the command and still matches the Sigma rule.
